---
bibliography: ../references.bib
---

# Methods

## Data Compilation

The `r species_label` assessment draws on fishery catch and effort,
size-composition, tagging, biological, and abundance-index data. The final data
period currently needs to be confirmed; the report configuration records the
latest data year as `r latest_data_year`.

The final report should identify the years included for each input data stream,
the date of the data extraction or database freeze, and any incomplete recent
periods excluded from model fitting. Data screening, expansion, aggregation,
and weighting rules should be traceable to the accepted `r assessment_year`
input files.

> **TODO:** Document the final data sources, screening rules, expansion methods,
> and any differences from the most recent assessment.

## Fishery Definitions

Model fisheries should be defined by gear, flag or fleet grouping, region,
selectivity group, and tag-recapture group where applicable.

Where index fisheries are used, the report should distinguish them from
extraction fisheries and state how they contribute abundance-index information.
The fishery definition table should show gear/fleet definitions, regional
assignment, index-fishery grouping, selectivity grouping, and tag-recapture or
reporting-rate groupings.

> **TODO:** Insert the final fishery definition table from the input-preparation
> pipeline.

## Abundance Indices

The abundance-index section should describe nominal and standardised CPUE
series, index fishery definitions, variance treatment, and any covariates or
spatial models used outside the assessment model.

Standardised CPUE analyses should be summarised with the data filters,
spatial-temporal model structure, covariates, index regions, and variance
treatment used for the accepted assessment. Where sdmTMB or another external
standardisation workflow is used, the report should cite the supporting
analysis and describe how index uncertainties are passed to MFCL
[@anderson_sdmtmb_2022; @teears_cpue_2023].

> **TODO:** Summarise the final CPUE analysis and link to supporting papers or
> analysis outputs.

## Size Composition and Biological Data

Length-frequency, weight-frequency, and age data should be described with their
filtering, weighting, sample-size treatment, and reporting units.

The final report should state whether length- and weight-composition data are
used simultaneously for any fisheries, how sample sizes are scaled, how
composition data are weighted, and whether conditional age-at-length
observations are included as a separate likelihood component for growth
estimation.

> **TODO:** Add final sample-size summaries and data-weighting settings.

## Tagging Data

When tagging data are included, this section should describe release programs,
recapture processing, tag-reporting groups, tagger effects, mixing assumptions,
and tag likelihood settings.

The final report should identify which tagging programs are included, how
release and recapture records are processed, and how tag loss, unusable
recaptures, tagger effects, tag shedding, initial mortality, mixing period,
tag-reporting rates, and recapture-region treatments are represented in the
model.

> **TODO:** Confirm the final tagging treatment for the `r assessment_year`
> model.

## Model Description

The assessment model consists of linked components for population dynamics,
fishery dynamics, tagged-fish dynamics, observation models, parameter
estimation, and stock-status interpretation. Technical details of the MFCL
formulation are given by @hampton_spatially-disaggregated_2001 and
@kleiber_multifan-cl_2019.

The population is partitioned into spatial regions and quarterly age classes,
with the oldest age class treated as a plus group. Recruitment is defined as the
appearance of age-class 1 fish in the population and is modelled at quarterly
time steps. A Beverton-Holt stock-recruitment relationship is used primarily so
that yield analyses, projections, and depletion-based reference points can be
calculated [@francis_use_1992; @harley_preliminary_2011]. In WCPO tuna
assessments, the stock-recruitment relationship is generally given a weak
penalty so that it has little influence on estimated historical recruitment
while still supporting reference-point calculations [@ianelli_independent_2012].

Growth, maturity, natural mortality, movement, selectivity, tag-reporting rates,
and fishery catchability are specified or estimated according to the selected
model configuration. The final model description should state the number of
regions and quarterly age classes, the model time window, the oldest-age plus
group, recruitment settings, initial-population treatment, stock-recruitment
steepness values, penalty weights, and whether the model is catch-conditioned
or uses catch-error dynamics.

> **TODO:** Add final software versions, executable or backend details, model
> time window, regional structure, age classes, parameter phases, control flags,
> and model-control settings used for the selected model set.

## Observation Models and Likelihood Components

The objective function combines likelihood components for fishery data,
abundance indices, size composition, tagging data, conditional age-at-length
data where used, and penalty terms. Catch-conditioned MFCL models treat catch as
known and solve for fishing mortality, reducing the number of effort-deviation
parameters relative to earlier catch-errors models. Index fisheries can then
contribute a CPUE likelihood component.

Size-composition and conditional age-at-length data are usually down-weighted
to account for non-independence and sampling processes that are not fully
represented by the multinomial likelihood. Tag-recapture data are often fitted
with an overdispersed likelihood because tag returns can be more variable than a
simple Poisson process.

The accepted likelihood settings should be checked against CPUE fits,
composition fits, tagging diagnostics, and model convergence diagnostics. Any
alternative composition-weighting methods or likelihood formulations considered
during model development should be described in the sensitivity section.

> **TODO:** Confirm the final likelihood components, data weights, CPUE
> variances, tag likelihood settings, and any penalties or bounds used in the
> `r assessment_year` model.

## Model Runs

The model-run section should separate diagnostic or base models, stepwise model
development, one-off sensitivities, and the structural uncertainty grid.

The `r assessment_year` model-run registry should include objective function
values, gradients, Hessian status, jitter counts, restart notes, and evidence
that key management quantities are stable. If a model is retained despite a
non-positive-definite Hessian or related warning, the report should explain the
diagnostic evidence supporting that decision.

> **TODO:** List the final diagnostic model, sensitivity runs, uncertainty axes,
> and model-weighting approach. Model registries are inserted in the Tables
> section when the report is rendered from selected analysis outputs.

## Stock Assessment Interpretation

Dynamic depletion is calculated by comparing estimated spawning potential in
each time period with the spawning potential expected under a no-fishing
scenario, usually written as $SB_t / SB_{F=0,t}$. This approach accounts for
time-varying recruitment in both the fished and unfished trajectories and is
therefore more informative than comparing recent spawning potential with an
initial biomass level that may not be representative.

The WCPFC limit reference point for bigeye tuna is based on 20 percent of
unfished spawning potential. The exact averaging period and recent-period
definitions must be updated for the `r assessment_year` assessment. Fishing
mortality reference quantities are derived from yield analysis, including
$F_{recent}/F_{MSY}$, and dynamic Kobe or Majuro plots can be produced by
repeating the yield calculations through time [@kleiber_multifan-cl_2019].

The `r assessment_year` report should state the no-fishing reference period,
recent-depletion period, recent fishing-mortality period, and any management
objective against which stock status is interpreted. If terminal years are
excluded from recent averages, the reason should be stated explicitly.

> **TODO:** Update the no-fishing reference period, recent-period definition,
> target or management objective text, yield-analysis settings, and projection
> assumptions once the final management-advice workflow is agreed.
